key_insight: A variational nonlocal phase-field framework models dynamic fracture without explicit crack tracking, using nonlocal kinematics and a kernel-based crack-surface functional that recovers local behavior as the interaction domain shrinks.
engineering_relevance: It provides a more flexible and variationally consistent tool for simulating crack initiation, branching, and fragmentation in elastic solids under dynamic loading.
method: Nonlocal kinematics with kernel-dependent function spaces for weaker regularity requirements
Nonlocal crack-surface functional as an integral analogue of Ambrosio--Tortorelli regularization
Coupled nonlocal momentum balance and irreversible phase-field evolution solved by scalar auxiliary-variable or staggered finite-element schemes
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful simulations of Mode-I fracture, dynamic branching, Kalthoff--Winkler-type shear fracture, and fragmentation, with crack-tip speeds below 0.6c_R and an inclined shear crack path of about 48°.
watch_item: Accuracy and physical fidelity depend on the chosen nonlocal interaction kernel/domain, so calibration and mesh/time-step sensitivity should be checked carefully.
paper_title: A Variational Nonlocal Phase-Field Model for Dynamic Fracture in Elastic Solids
